description: I am not a top-level administrator for Paychex Flex, so my usage is more limited to our specific branch and ensuring paychecks are accurate, coordinating with our main branch in Canada to ensure the AP department has released paychecks not just on-time, but early, which has allowed Paychex Flex to deposit some employees checks as early as Wednesday. From an employee-usage side, it's a great way to easily access pay stubs, W-2s, retirement funds, and more, with almost zero hassle.
author: Nicholas D.
pros: I love Paychex Flex for, dare I say, it's flexibility. It's allowed us to set up employees who work and live in the same state, in different states, ones who travel essentially year-round, and more, and have the taxes squared away for us. It's great for users as well, as it allows us, as users, to manage retirement (401k), manage direct deposit, and even manage taxes, right from the employee login. We don't use time clocks, so no time tracking is needed. Customer support has been amazing, helping solve issues as soon as something pops up. Great value for what it provides. It's extremely easy to view and pay bills, as well as employees.
cons: The taxes calculated aren't perfect for cross-state employees, but it's very close. We don't use time clocks, as we're all salaried, so no tracking needed, but the communication features are somewhat lacking, and we do not use any to communicate with employees, rather doing so via phone calls or in-person.

description: i believe i had a good experience with paycheck flex, nothing negative to talk about or say regarding company wise .
author: kattya B.
pros: paycheck flex was very easy to use with my prior employer never had any issues, and if I did have any questions customer support was very friendly and responded very quickly.
cons: nothing really, paychex flex was very easy to use and only downfall would be the employer at the time.

description: I was very unhappy and the amount of times there were errors or late release of payroll was an ongoing concern. With banks releasing funds 1-2 days early I always would spend hours explaining to staff that it was not us but the payroll service that had to release the funds and generally released them very late in the week although my payroll was completed on Mondays
author: Tracy W.
pros: The staff that I encountered was very helpful and always assisted when possible, it just always seems I had always talked to someone that may not have been as knowledgeable about the procedures and processes to correct problems
cons: The pricing and the length of time it took to resolve issues with payroll. In my time working with them I rarely ever encountered anyone who was able to immediately fix an issue without involving other staff members
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

author: David A.
pros: Paychex Flex was not necessarily an easy to use product, but almost easy navigating around. Unfortunately, we did run into a lot of issues with the product and the support was lacking. Once you got to the right place, it was easier.
cons: Honestly, everything about Paychex Flex is lacking. Unfortunately, there software is lacking coming into the new age and it extremely outdated. The User interface is just a bunch of different modules integrated with eachother and not really user friendly. For the money, I would spend a little more and go elsewhere.

description: It's become the perfect option for our small businesses by automating tax data, self-service access via desktop or mobile app, and all the additional workforce management tasks. We couldn't be more pleased!
author: Mai M.
pros: We love how fast and automated payroll processing has become for our employees with direct deposit and same-day payment.
cons: No cons! Our employees find it super simple whether on desktop or the app! It's been a super easy transition for our employees.

review_title: Great payroll product for small companies.
description: Paychex Flex.is a great option for a small to medium sized company. Software was easy to use and understand, and their customer service was top notch.
author: William B.
pros: Easy to use, and fairly affordable. The Flex software worked great. Perfect.payroll solution for small to midsized companies.
cons: The pricing continued.to increase every year until it was unaffordable. Set up process was a bit complicated, as well as the process of leaving the service.

description: The navigating process is simple and easy to utilize. All of our employees can manage and track their time, and access the new PEO benefits beginning on 04/01/26.
author: Zameeya J.
pros: Ease of use and I love that I can keep track of my payment schedule and have access to all of my hours worked and documents. Also, a new feature has been introduced to our practice. The PEO benefit where we as employees can take arrange if empire benefits, 401K, Health insurance and so forth.
cons: There is nothing to dislike. I find PayChex to be very useful within our healthcare practice and it works really well.

description: My overall experience was good. This software was a great tool and I loved that it was cloud based as a manager.
author: Naombi S.
pros: Easy to use and navigate at times. The setup was manageable and allowed for easy learning and training not only for me as the manager, but training other employees to use it as well.
cons: Sometimes, there were quirks and hang ups. It would get frozen, lock employees out or just not work when trying to onboard new employees.

review_title: Easy to use and a great value for a small organization!
description: It's a positive experience. Good value with an easy interface. Great for a small organization. Easy to make updates.
author: Barbara G.
pros: Very easy to use for a small organization plus it's a good value. Very intuitive so you can pretty much figure out everything yourself. Helpful customer support. Makes all the tax allocations by state easy.
cons: Some of the functionalities have different support teams, and the teams aren't cross-trained - so you can wait on hold only to be told that they need to transfer you elsewhere. My dedicated rep is never available and hold times can be very long for general support.

author: Verified Reviewer
pros: Paychex Flex was a solid solution for simple, basic time-tracking, payroll, and employee benefits management. Rare downtime or issues with software.
cons: The UI felt a bit dated, and some users reported having trouble finding specific things within their portal.

description: We love the features in Flex and it is fairly easy to use once you are trained. With employees coming and going we need a provider with an easy learning curve.
author: Pam S.
pros: Good customer support and dedicated rep. Payroll is easy to use and the time keeping is nice for our employees with the app. It is a good value for the money because we are a non-profit and they give us a nice discount.
cons: Reporting could be a bit better but they work with you to customize if you need that. We do get new reps quite often but the are always a phone call or e-mail away when needed fast during payroll.

description: Overall, it has been a great experience making the lives of the management/HR much more productive and streamlined. Billing and payment are both available but have only used the payment side of things.
author: Mattias A.
pros: Paychex Flex was a great secure way to manage pay stubs for employees that could access at any time with an ease of use UI and great customer support.
cons: The app would occasionally have a "cooke" or cache bug of some sort. Never really figured out what it was, but it was a minor inconvenience if anything that we had to tell each employee to force stop the app/clear cache to log in again successfully.

description: I feel like it is a slow going transition to Paychex. The system isn't totally user friendly, you need training to use it. They made promises that they have not kept yet. Still working on it.
author: Lisa R.
pros: I like the app that the employees use to log in their time. Clock in/clock out and that is shows me the location when they punch in/out.
cons: I am unable to pull a report showing the punch times and locations. Their report shows coordinates of their location, totally useless.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

description: It's been good so far. It's not easy to use when adding a new hire. Uploading and signing documents is not easy to navigate.
author: Tiffany S.
pros: I use the admin section. I personally like that I am able to approve my teams hours and that I can break it down by pay week or pay period.
cons: When there are punch in/out errors from an employee, there's no option to print the individual error or the full time week itself which is needed for my business because we need signatures approving the adjustment from the error.

description: It has been simple and easy to learn. The reporting features are great but would use some improvement for example tracking call offs and leave management.
author: Felicia A.
pros: I like the ability to streamline time and attendance with onboarding and employee information. The reporting feature is also a great add.
cons: The clocks we are using have been problematic, specifically the camera. The sensitivity has caused many enrollment issues. I am also not a fan of the change to the new timecard layout

review_title: Dependable Payroll and HR Solution with a User-Friendly Interface
description: Overall, Paychex Flex has been a dependable solution for managing payroll and HR tasks. It provides the core functionality needed in a clear, user-friendly way while offering additional features that grow with the business. While not perfect, it strikes a good balance between ease of use and functionality.
author: Jasmine M.
pros: Paychex Flex offers an intuitive interface that makes payroll and HR management straightforward. The platform is reliable, and the mobile app is especially helpful for accessing pay stubs, tax forms, and benefits on the go. It reduces paperwork and streamlines processes, which saves time for both employees and administrators.
cons: Some pages can be slow to load during peak times, and a few of the more advanced features—such as detailed reporting and benefits breakdowns—require extra time to learn. Customization options could also be expanded to improve usability for different business needs.

description: Overall, it wasn't a positive experience. However, in the end it wasn't my decision, it was my supervisors. But I didn't feel like it was worth the money that we spent.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: It was pretty easy to use. The platform was straight forward and I never had an issue processing payroll. They also offered pretty comprehensive training.
cons: It was very difficult to ever get a response for any payroll or HR related questions. No one would answer my call and it took days to get a response. It was also a very expensive product to use and had sub-par health insurance.

review_title: Paychex is easy to use but still pricey for small biz
description: At times it was great and at times very frustrating. The cost structure is frustrating. At times they increase costs without notification
author: Christina E.
pros: Ease of use is great. I think anybody can learn to use it themselves. I also like the features in the app
cons: The cost seems too high for a small business for what you get. I'd like to see a more affordable option for businesses with less than 15 people.

description: My overall experience is pretty positive. Its easy to manage time and money as well as access benefits.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: Paychex Flex has a user friendly interface and abilities to track and adjust payroll in real time. It is also easy to access benefits.
cons: Mobile version is clunky. During use, the system seems a little message heavy, disrupting focus on necessary work flow.

description: Overall I am happy with it. However, I did take a sick day once and I was not paid for it. I am not sure if that was a manager or system failure.
author: Elizabeth H.
pros: I like that i can see the hours i am working in real time. so that I can directly see what i am going to be paid for that week.
cons: i don't like how you cannot sign in or check in from your phone, you have to either log in on computer or physical box in spa.

description: It does its job but I am looking for a new payroll provider due to the paint points we experience with paychex
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: Ease of use - its pretty generic/basic, and user friendly. the layout is all clean cut, including the user interface
cons: customer service and customer support is poor. we have a new rep almost ever week. it is never consistent. it is also very high in price considering we barely have any features

description: I only used Paychex Flex for a short amount of time, as my company decided to switch to a different product
author: Colby W.
pros: It was easy to download my pay statements without a lot of hoops to jump through. The pay statements were easy to read
cons: Logging in would take forever and once in, it was difficult to navigate through their system to find my paycheck
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

description: It's better than some apps I've used but not as good as others.
author: Joan K.
pros: The reports are good and easy to customize.
cons: The modules work independently of each other. One should be able to enter information in one location and have it 'spread out' to all other locations. This does not happen so you often have to enter the same information in multiple locations.

review_title: On the fence about this product.
description: My overall experience with Paychex Flex is that it is an OK value for your money taking all considerations aside.
author: Clarice L.
pros: What I liked most about Paychex Flex was the Customer Support, Ease of Use, Time Tracking, Communication, Clock and Timer.
cons: What I liked least about Paychex Flex was the bugs and issues that arise that require you to contact customer service.

description: Any time anywhere I have access to the app. its convenient and I can do it all in one app. Training done easy.
author: Sonia O.
pros: easy access to my information. all in one deal. I don't need to hire a different company for trainings
cons: the double authentication log in. It makes it difficult when I am in a hurry. I cant tell you how many times Ive asked it to remember me.
